description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest image with the docker:dind service and sets DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay. Include a global before_script that runs `docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_BUILD_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY`, sets `NAME="$CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$(echo $CI_COMMIT_SHA|cut -c1-8)"`, and sets `NAME_BUILDER="${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:builder-$(cat npm-shrinkwrap.json package.json|sha1sum|cut -c1-8)"`. In the build stage add a build job that executes `set -euo pipefail`, then `docker pull "${NAME_BUILDER}" || docker build -t "${NAME_BUILDER}" . && docker push "${NAME_BUILDER}"`, then `echo -e "FROM ${NAME_BUILDER}\n$(cat Dockerfile.build)" > Dockerfile.tmp`, then `docker build -t "${NAME}" -f Dockerfile.tmp .`, and finally `docker push "${NAME}"`. In the deploy stage add a deploy_prod job that runs only on the prod branch and runs `docker run -e "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID" -e "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" --entrypoint ./deploy.sh "$NAME" dist/ latency.at`. Also add a deploy_stage job that runs only on the master branch and runs `docker run -e "AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID" -e "AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY" --entrypoint ./deploy.sh "$NAME" dist/ dev.latency.at`.
